benchmark_report.methodology: Random Scientific Selection with Statistical Demolition

lines_vs_complexity.interpretation: Strong positive correlation: More lines = More complexity

complexity_vs_usability.interpretation: Strong negative correlation: Higher complexity = Lower usability

mcp_dependencies_vs_token_cost.interpretation: More MCP calls = Higher token costs

evidence: 664 lines of 'thinking' that thinks itself into paralysis
29 MCP dependency calls - more dependencies than a JavaScript project
Claims 'maximum capability' but delivers maximum confusion
Sequential thinking so sequential it forgot to be useful
Complexity score 4.8/5 - academic masturbation achievement unlocked
ultrathink-full-mcp.md.statistical_roast: This command consumes 18,173 tokens to accomplish what a human brain does in 0 tokens. ROI: -∞
ultrathink-full-mcp.md.predicted_user_behavior: 95% will abandon after reading first 50 lines
ultrathink-full-mcp.md.comparison: Like using a nuclear reactor to charge your phone - technically possible, economically insane

evidence: 498 lines to route between 3 thinking modes
4 orchestration patterns to solve a problem that doesn't exist
Complexity score 4.6/5 - PhD in missing the point
Uses complexity to manage complexity - peak irony achieved
adaptive-complexity-router.md.statistical_roast: Spends 11,274 tokens to decide how to think. Humans have been thinking for free for 200,000 years.
adaptive-complexity-router.md.predicted_user_behavior: Will use manual thinking instead of this thinking about thinking
adaptive-complexity-router.md.comparison: Like having a committee to decide how to form committees

evidence: 456 lines to help ADHD brains focus - the irony is palpable
7 session phases for people who struggle with task switching
Complex boundary enforcement for simplicity seekers
Pattern capture systems more complex than the patterns themselves
adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.statistical_roast: 12,111 tokens to help people who get overwhelmed by complexity. Logic: -100%
adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.predicted_user_behavior: ADHD users will hyperfocus on understanding this instead of using it
adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.comparison: Like writing War and Peace to teach speed reading

evidence: 441 lines to enhance code while being unenhanced itself
4 enhancement strategies, 4 validation phases - strategy overload
Pattern learning systems that haven't learned to be simple
Complexity score 4.3/5 - enhancement failed on itself
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.statistical_roast: Uses 11,411 tokens to make code better while being a prime example of bad code
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.predicted_user_behavior: Will enhance code manually to avoid this enhancement complexity
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.comparison: Like a life coach who can't get their own life together

evidence: Only 249 lines - almost reasonable for this ecosystem
Rapid implementation that still requires thinking overhead
Complexity score 3.2/5 - barely failing the simplicity test
Delegates to other complex tools when things get complex
java-rapid-implementation.md.statistical_roast: 8,736 tokens for 'rapid' implementation. Rapid like continental drift.
java-rapid-implementation.md.predicted_user_behavior: Might actually use this one if desperate enough
java-rapid-implementation.md.comparison: Like the least broken tool in a shed full of broken tools

complexity_entropy.interpretation: High entropy = High unpredictability of usefulness

immediate_actions: Apply the 3-question rule to the tools themselves
Remember: Tools should solve problems, not create them
Consider manual implementation as the default choice
design_principles: Lines of code is inversely proportional to usability
Complexity should be hidden, not multiplied
Token consumption should be justified by value
Cognitive load must stay under 7 items
quality_gates: If it takes longer to understand than to implement manually: DELETE
If it costs more than hiring a developer: DELETE
If it has more thinking blocks than actual code: DELETE
If you need a PhD to use it: DELETE

statistical_significance.conclusion: These commands are statistically significantly worse than doing nothing
